page.content: In Q1 2024, HSBC Holdings reported key metrics indicating a total revenue of HKD1.5B, net income of HKD600M, and operating costs of HKD100M, resulting in total expenses of HKD2.5B and a gross profit of HKD900M.

lines.text: In Q1 2024, HSBC Holdings reported key metrics indicating a total revenue of HKD1.5B, net income of HKD600M, and
operating costs of HKD100M, resulting in total expenses of HKD2.5B and a gross profit of HKD900M.

page.content: The revenue breakdown showcased interest income of HKD150M, fees and commissions of HKD200M, and trading income of HKD300M, leading to an EBITDA of HKD900M.

lines.text: The revenue breakdown showcased interest income of HKD150M, fees and commissions of HKD200M, and trading income of
HKD300M, leading to an EBITDA of HKD900M.

page.content: The net profit margin stood at 15.0%, return on assets at 2.5%, and return on equity at 12.0%, with net income growth at 20%. Key ratios revealed a cost-to-income ratio of 45%, tier 1 capital of 12.5%, liquidity ratio at 150%, loan-to-deposit ratio of 80%, market share at 25%, and customer deposits totaling HKD10B. Commercial loans amounted to HKD5B, retail loans to HKD8B, and the investment portfolio reached HKD4B, while risk-weighted assets were HKD5B, total assets at HKD40B, and total liabilities at HKD80B. In the non-performing loans overview, corporate loans were valued at HKD300M with a ratio of 2.5% and status as under review, retail loans at HKD150M with a ratio of 1.8% and stable status, mortgages at HKD200M with a ratio of 1.6% and improving status, small business loans at HKD100M with a ratio of 3.0% and critical status, and total NPLs reaching HKD850M with an overall status of 2.2%.
